articles.title: NASA's Swift Satellite Needs a Rescue Mission
articles.summary: NASA's Swift satellite is an old space telescope that studies powerful explosions in space called gamma-ray bursts. It has been working for over 20 years, but now it is slowly falling out of orbit and could crash to Earth soon. A company named Katalyst Space Technologies is trying to save it by sending a robot spacecraft called Link to meet Swift in space. Link will dock with Swift and push it higher to keep it safe. This rescue mission is very tricky because Swift was not made to be captured, and Katalyst has never done this before. They must launch by June to have enough time before Swift gets too low. If successful, Swift can continue its important science work, helping scientists learn more about the universe.

articles.title: People Often Guess Wrong About Food and the Planet
articles.summary: A new study found that many people do not know which foods are good or bad for the environment. Researchers asked 168 people in the UK to sort foods from a supermarket into groups based on how they think the foods affect the planet. The results showed that people often make mistakes. For example, they thought meat and processed foods are always very harmful, but sometimes plant-based foods like nuts can also have a big impact. The study suggests that putting simple labels on food, like grades from A to E, could help people make better choices to protect the environment. This is important because what we eat can change the climate and nature. The researchers hope that with better information, everyone can help the Earth by choosing foods wisely.

articles.title: Tiny Plastic Bits Fall from the Sky into Forests
articles.summary: Scientists found that tiny plastic pieces called microplastics are falling from the sky and landing in forests. These plastics come from the air and settle on tree leaves. When it rains or leaves fall in autumn, the plastics move to the forest soil. The study shows that forests are getting polluted this way, which is a new problem. The researchers collected samples from forests in Germany to learn more. They say forests can help us understand how much plastic is in the air. This pollution might harm forests and could even affect the air we breathe. It's important to study this to protect our environment.

articles.title: A New Blood Test to Find Pancreatic Cancer Early
articles.summary: Scientists have made a new blood test to help find pancreatic cancer sooner. Pancreatic cancer is a very serious disease that is often found too late, making it hard to treat. This test looks for special signs in the blood called biomarkers. It uses four markers, including two new ones, to check if someone has the cancer. In tests, it correctly found cancer about 92% of the time and could detect early stages too. This could help doctors catch the cancer earlier when treatment works better, saving more lives. The test also helps tell cancer apart from other health problems like pancreatitis, so people don't get worried for no reason. More studies are needed to see if it can be used for screening people at high risk.

articles.title: A Liver Enzyme and Cancer Risk
articles.summary: Scientists found that blocking an enzyme called Caspase-2 might not be safe for treating fatty liver disease. They studied mice and discovered that when this enzyme is missing, liver cells grow too much and become damaged. Over time, this can lead to liver cancer. The research shows that while stopping Caspase-2 might help young animals or prevent fatty liver for a short time, it causes problems as they get older. This means doctors need to be careful when developing new treatments. The study was done at the University of Adelaide and published in a science journal.

articles.title: A Debate About Heathrow Airport's Big Plan
articles.summary: Heathrow Airport in London wants to grow bigger by adding a new runway and other buildings. This plan costs £49 billion and might change roads like the M25. Jack Rankin, a local MP, says he is against this expansion because his area is already too crowded with traffic and noise from planes. He worries that more planes and cars will make life harder for people living nearby. However, he supports a new train link to the airport to help with transportation. The airport and government say expansion is urgent because Heathrow is full and needs to grow to help businesses and create jobs. Business groups agree it will bring benefits, but some people are concerned about the environment and local life.
